english: one's daughters Dzl.; to conjure up, ghosts, des bdag _gúg-par _gyúr-ro (des bdag 'gug par 'gyur ro) by this (charm) I may be conjured up; blo náṅ-du _gúg-la (blo nang du 'gug la) calling the spirit back into its inner domain, abstracting the mind from the external world. — 4. to draw back, to cause to return, to convey back Mil., C.
pf. gud (gud), = rgud-pa (rgud pa)? gúd-du bčúg-pa (gud du bcug pa) to ruin, to reduce to an extremity Schr.; rtsa byiṅ-gúd (rtsa bying gud) dal Med. a pulse slow and sinking.

1. pf. gum (gum), _gums ('gums) eleg. to die. — 2. pf. bkum (bkum), fut. dkum (dkum), imp. kum(s) (kum(s)), to kill, to put to death Dzl. frq.; to slaughter (butcher), gsár-du bkúm-pai śa (gsar du bkum pai sha), meat of an animal just killed, fresh meat Dzl. — 3. to bend, curve, make crooked, to contract, v. kum (kum) and skúm-pa (skum pa).

(cf. sgul-ba (sgul ba)) to change place or posture, to move, shake, to be agitated, *ri-gu ḍód-pa-la gul dug* (*ri gu 'dod pa la gul dug*) the kid moves in the womb (of the goat); _gul-dká ('gul dka) (the limb) moves with difficulty Med. frq.; _gul yaṅ ma nús-so ('gul yang ma nus so) (they) would not even stir (from terror) Dzl.; to waver, tremble, shiver, _dár-źiṅ _gúl-ba ('dar zhing 'gul ba); sa-_gúl (sa 'gul) (pronounced *saṅ-gúl* (*sang gul*)) earthquake W.

pf. bkag (bkag), fut. dgag (dgag), imp. kog (kog) to hinder, prohibit, stop, bdág-gis bkág-na yaṅ ma túb-kyis (bdag gis bkag na yang ma thub kyis) though I was preventing it, I could not (carry my point) Dzl.; ma bkág-ste náṅ-du btaṅ (ma bkag ste nang du btang) he admitted him without impediment Dzl.; *kág-če med zér-k̇an-gyi ka-śóg* (*kag ce med zer mkhan gyi ka shog*) a warrant, a permit to traffic without hinderance, a pass-bill, and the like W.; to shut, to lock (up), to close, sgo (sgo) the door Glr., lam (lam) the road frq., to close one's nose with the hand Pth.; to retain, keep back excretions Med., bśaṅ-dgág (bshang dgag) obstruction (cf. _gag ('gag)); *zá-če kág-te śi* (*za ce kag te shi*) W. his food sticking fast he died; to lock up, shut up (things for keeping), to pen up (sheep, cattle), *kág-te bór-če* (*kag te bor ce*) W. id; dgag-dbyé (dgag dbye) the ending of the seclusion; viz. of the monks who have to stay in their houses during the rainy season Schf., Tar. 10, 10, cf. Köpp. I, 369; to forbid, dgag-sgrúb (dgag sgrub) Sch.: 'to forbid and to allow'(?); gág-pai sgra (gag pai sgra), _gag-tsíg ('gag tshig) a prohibitive particle Gram.; bkág-ča byéd-pa (bkag ca byed pa) to forbid, prohibit Sch.; *k̇a kág-če* (*kha kag ce*) W. to silence, to hush; dgág-pa (dgag pa) a negative, a negation; bkág-ča (bkag ca) the negative side Was. (282).

pf. bkaṅ (bkang), fut. dgaṅ (dgang), imp. koṅ (kong) 1. to fill, tib-ril čus (tib ril cus) or (seld.) čú-las (cu las), or tib-ril-du čus (tib ril du cus), or ču (cu), (to fill) a tea-pot with water; to soil, smear, stain, the bed with blood Glr.; dgaṅ-dka (dgang dka) difficult to be filled, not to be satisfied, insatiable Stg. — 2. to fulfil (more frq. skóṅ-ba (skong ba)) tugs-dám (thugs dam) Lex. — 3. gźu _géns-pa (gzhu 'gens pa), mda _géns-pa (mda 'gens pa) to prepare bow and arrows for shooting, frq.; *tú-pag káṅ-če* (*thu pag kang ce*) W. to load a gun.

pf. bkab (bkab), fut. dgab (dgab), imp. kob (kob) (W. *bkob* (*bkob*)), to cover, e.g. one's breast with the hand; to cover up, ka (ka) an opening, aperture; to spread over or on, to set up, to put on, a cover, lid, cork, plug etc.; to protect, btsún-mo mi-ma-yin-gyis _gébs-su _júg-pa (btsun mo mi ma yin gyis 'gebs su 'jug pa) to have the queen protected by ghosts; to disguise, metaph: *bkáb-ste* (*bkab ste*) in disguised language, euphemistically W., *káb-če p̣i-la* (*kab ce phi la*) in order to express it euphemistically.

acc. to Cs. another form for _gúm-pa ('gum pa), to kill, to destroy; Schr.: klád-pa _géms-pa (klad pa 'gems pa) to surprise; to overthrow an argument by reason; cf. mgo-_géms (mgo 'gems) Lex. w.e.; as a partic.: stupid Schr.; the few passages, where I met with the word, leave its meaning doubtful.

pf. bkal (bkal), fut. dgal (dgal), imp. kol (kol), 1. to load, to lay on a burden, brui k̇al čig bkál-te (brui khal cig bkal te) loaded with a load of grain Dzl.; fig. to put a yoke upon a person's neck, byur (byur) to bring down misery on a person; W. to bring accusations against a person, *mi _ós-pe lás-ka źig mi źig-la kal táṅ-na* (*mi 'os pe las ka zhig mi zhig la kal tang na*) Ld. if one is accused of an unlawful action; k̇ral _gél-ba (khral 'gel ba) to impose <
